introduction.text: You know the Golden Rules. Now, let's look at the tricky pairs: 2s, 3s, 4s, 6s, 7s, and 9s. The rule here is context. Look at MY card. If I am weak, you Split to attack. If I am strong, do not Split – just play it like a normal hand.

sequence.text: You have two 9s. Total 18. Dealer has a 7. Think! If dealer has 10 underneath, he has 17. Your 18 beats 17. Why risk splitting? Just Take the money. STAND.

finish.text: Smart. I had 17. You had 18. Easy win without the stress of splitting.

sequence.text: Now I have a 6 (Weak). You have 18. Winning TWO bets is better. Split the 9s to punish my weak card.
Okay, look at Hand 1 (Left). You have 19. That is a winning score. Don't risk it. Finish this hand first.
Now focus on Hand 2 (Right). You have 11 vs 6. This is the moment to attack. Double Down on the second hand!

finish.text: Jackpot! Hand 1 won (19 vs 21 bust). Hand 2 doubled and hit 21. Perfect play.

scenarios.title: Small Pairs vs Weak Dealer (2,2 vs 5)

sequence.text: Pair of 2s. Total 4. That's a terrible starting number. But I have a 5. Split them! Turn one bad hand (4) into two decent starts.
Hand 2 is 10 vs 5. Double Down again!

finish.text: I busted. Since you split and doubled both, you win 4x your bet. Massive profit.

scenarios.title: Small Pairs vs Strong Dealer (6,6 vs 8)
scenarios.goal: Teach HITTING (not splitting) pairs when dealer is strong.

sequence.text: Two 6s. Total 12. Dealer has 8. DANGER. If you split, you start two hands with 6 against an 8. You will likely lose both. Just Hit the 12 like a normal Hard Hand.
You saved yourself! You drew a 7 and now have 19. Secure the win. STAND.

finish.text: Good decision. You got 19 and beat my 18. If you had split, you would have started with 6s and likely lost both hands.

scenarios.title: The 4,4 Exception (4,4 vs 3)
scenarios.goal: Teach that 4,4 is usually a Hit (unless table says otherwise).

sequence.text: Two 4s. Total 8. Should we split? No. 4 is a terrible starting card. 8 is a decent starting number. Don't split 4s here. Just Hit.
You got 11! Normally you would Double. But you already hit once! The rules say: No Double after Hit. You must Hit again.

finish.text: You got 21 and I busted. Hitting the 4s was the right call.

conclusion.text: Congratulations! You have finished the Basic Strategy Course. You know every button and every decision. But... is it possible to know what card comes next?
